public void addNum(int val) {

   // Check if val is already in any interval

   int[] newInterval = {val, val};

   int index = findInsertPosition(val);

   // Check the interval before index

   if (index > 0) {

       int[] prev = intervals.get(index - 1);

       if (prev[0] <= val && val <= prev[1]) {

           return; // already covered

       }

   }

   if (index < intervals.size()) {

       int[] next = intervals.get(index);

       if (next[0] <= val && val <= next[1]) {

           return; // already covered

       }

   }

   // Now, insert the new interval and merge if needed

   intervals.add(index, newInterval);

   // Merge with previous if possible

   if (index > 0 && intervals.get(index - 1)[1] + 1 >= intervals.get(index)[0]) {

       mergeIntervals(index - 1, index);

   }

   // Merge with next if possible

   if (index < intervals.size() - 1 && intervals.get(index)[1] + 1 >= intervals.get(index + 1)[0]) {

       mergeIntervals(index, index + 1);

   }

}

public int findInsertPosition(int val) {

   int left = 0, right = intervals.size() - 1;

}

class SummaryRanges {

    private List<int[]> intervals;

    public SummaryRanges() {

        intervals = new ArrayList<>();

    }

    public void addNum(int value) {

        // Find the index to insert the new interval [value, value]

        int index = findIndex(value);

        // Check if already covered by previous interval

        if (index > 0 && intervals.get(index - 1)[1] >= value) {

            return;

        }

        // Insert new interval

        intervals.add(index, new int[]{value, value});

        // Merge with previous if possible

        if (index > 0 && intervals.get(index - 1)[1] + 1 == intervals.get(index)[0]) {

            // Merge

            int newStart = intervals.get(index - 1)[0];

            int newEnd = intervals.get(index)[1];

            intervals.set(index - 1, new int[]{newStart, newEnd});

            intervals.remove(index);

            index = index - 1;

            // Now check if this merged interval can merge with next

            if (index < intervals.size() - 1 && intervals.get(index)[1] + 1 == intervals.get(index + 1)[0]) {

                int newStart2 = intervals.get(index)[0];

                int newEnd2 = intervals.get(index + 1)[1];

                intervals.set(index, new int[]{newStart2, newEnd2});

                intervals.remove(index + 1);

            }

        }

        // Merge with next if possible

        if (index < intervals.size() - 1 && intervals.get(index)[1] + 1 == intervals.get(index + 1)[0]) {

            int newStart = intervals.get(index)[0];

            int newEnd = intervals.get(index + 1)[1];

            intervals.set(index, new int[]{newStart, newEnd});

            intervals.remove(index + 1);

        }

    }

    private int findIndex(int value) {

        int left = 0, right = intervals.size() - 1;

        while (left <= right) {

            int mid = (left + right) / 2;

            if (intervals.get(mid)[0] <= value) {

                left = mid + 1;

            } else {

                right = mid - 1;

            }

        }

        return left;

    }

    public int[][] getIntervals() {

        return intervals.toArray(new int[0][]);

    }

}

The SummaryRanges class will have a list of intervals.

But in Java, the list is a List<int[]>. 

Now, the code:

class SummaryRanges {

    private List<int[]> intervals;

    public SummaryRanges() {

        intervals = new ArrayList<>();

    }

    public void addNum(int value) {

        // Find the index to insert the new interval [value, value]

        int index = findIndex(value);

        // Check if already covered by previous interval

        if (index > 0 && intervals.get(index - 1)[1] >= value) {

            return;

        }

        // Insert new interval

        intervals.add(index, new int[]{value, value});

        // Merge with previous if possible

        if (index > 0 && intervals.get(index - 1)[1] + 1 == intervals.get(index)[0]) {

            // Merge

            int newStart = intervals.get(index - 1)[0];

            int newEnd = intervals.get(index)[1];

            intervals.set(index - 1, new int[]{newStart, newEnd});

            intervals.remove(index);

            index = index - 1;

            // Now check if this merged interval can merge with next

            if (index < intervals.size() - 1 && intervals.get(index)[1] + 1 == intervals.get(index + 1)[0]) {

                int newStart2 = intervals.get(index)[0];

                int newEnd2 = intervals.get(index + 1)[1];

                intervals.set(index, new int[]{newStart2, newEnd2});

                intervals.remove(index + 1);

            }

        }

        // Merge with next if possible

        if (index < intervals.size() - 1 && intervals.get(index)[1] + 1 == intervals.get(index + 1)[0]) {

            int newStart = intervals.get(index)[0];

            int newEnd = intervals.get(index + 1)[1];

            intervals.set(index, new int[]{newStart, newEnd});

            intervals.remove(index + 1);

        }

    }

    private int findIndex(int value) {

        int left = 0, right = intervals.size() - 1;

        while (left <= right) {

            int mid = (left + right) / 2;

            if (intervals.get(mid)[0] <= value) {

                left = mid + 1;

            } else {

                right = mid - 1;

            }

        }

        return left;

    }

    public int[][] getIntervals() {

        return intervals.toArray(new int[0][]);

    }

}
